 An improve nonlinear robust control approach for robotic manipulators with PSO-based global optimization strategy 

[Download PDF](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x.pdf)

[Download PDF](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x.pdf)

*   Article
*   [Open access](https://www.springernature.com/gp/open-research/about/the-fundamentals-of-open-access-and-open-research)
*   Published: 13 September 2024

An improve nonlinear robust control approach for robotic manipulators with PSO-based global optimization strategy
=================================================================================================================

*   [Peihao Yue](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#auth-Peihao-Yue-Aff1-Aff3)[1](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#Aff1),[3](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#Aff3), 
*   [Bowen Xu](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#auth-Bowen-Xu-Aff2)[2](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#Aff2)&
*   [Min Zhang](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#auth-Min-Zhang-Aff3)[3](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#Aff3)

[_Scientific Reports_](https://www.nature.com/srep)**volume 14**, Article number:21447 (2024) [Cite this article](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#citeas)

*   1760 Accesses

*   1 Citations

*   [Metrics details](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x/metrics)

Abstract
--------

During the trajectory tracking of robotic manipulators, many factors including dead zones, saturation, and uncertain dynamics, greatly increase the modeling and control difficulty. Aiming for this issue, a nonlinear active disturbance rejection control (NADRC)-based control strategy is proposed for robotic manipulators. In this controller, an extended state observer is introduced on basis of the dynamic model, to observe the extend state of model uncertainties and external disturbances. Then, in combination with the nonlinear feedback control structure, the robust trajectory tracking of robotic manipulators is achieved. Furthermore, to optimize the key parameters of the controller, an improved particle swarm optimization algorithm (IPSO) is designed using chaos theory, which improves the tracking accuracy of the proposed NDRC strategy effectively. Finally, using comparative studies, the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy is demonstrated by comparing with several commonly used controllers.

Article Open access 15 March 2025

Introduction
------------

With the continuous improvement of automation technology, robots have taken a more important role in industrial systems due to the flexible position movement pattern and strong adaptiveness to the work environment, and the phenomenon of “machines replacing people” has emerged in many fields, such as teleoperation, fault detection, health care, and other fields[1](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR1 "Wei, S. & Cong, S. Nonlinear computed torque control for a high-speed planar parallel manipulator. Mechatronics 19, 987–992 (2009)."),[2](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR2 "Pan, C. et al. A model-free output feedback control approach for the stabilization of underactuated TORA system with input saturation. Actuators 11(3), 97 (2022)."),[3](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR3 "Lewis, F., Abdallah, C. & Dawson, D. Control of robot manipulators (Macmillan, 1993)."). However, as a complex multi-input multi-output system, the robotic system is complex and highly nonlinear with various structural uncertainties, including dead zones, saturation, and uncertain dynamics, which can adversely affect the actuators[4](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR4 "Wang, Y. et al. LESO-based nonlinear continuous robust stabilization control of underactuated TORA systems. Actuators 11(8), 220 (2022)."),[5](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR5 "Xu, D., Xu, B., Hu, T. & Yin, L. Rules-reduced fuzzy neural network-based learning control for multiple constraints robots using online identification and compensation methods. Inf. Sci. 679, 121060 (2024)."). In actual practice, these factors cannot be ignored, making it difficult to establish an accurate modeling and control for the robotic manipulators[6](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR6 "Asada, H. & Slotine, J. Robot analysis and control (Wiley, 1986)."). Therefore, it is necessary to research on the anti-disturbance control strategy of robotic mechanism, to suppress the influence of the uncertainty and disturbances on tracking accuracy of servo system[7](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR7 "Liu J., Jian F., Liu J. Four Degrees of Freedom SCARA Robot Modeling and Simulation. In: International Symposium on Computer, Consumer and Control (IS3C 2014), Taichung, Taiwan, pp. 410–412, 2014."),[8](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR8 "Wang, C. et al. Nonlinear adaptive control of hydraulic system with observing and compensating mismatching uncertainties. IEEE Trans. Control Syst. Technol. 26, 927–938 (2018).").

In the beginning, considering the advantages of PID algorithm not relying on dynamic models and simple principles, PID was applied for the trajectory tracking of robots[9](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR9 "Thanh, T. & Ahn, K. Nonlinear PID control to improve the control performance of 2 axes pneumatic artificial muscle manipulator using neural network. Mechatronics 16, 577–587 (2006)."),[10](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR10 "Su, Y., Sun, D., Ren, L. & Mills, J. K. Integration of saturated PI synchronous control and PD feedback for control of parallel manipulators. IEEE Trans. Robot. 22, 202–207 (2006)."). When executing tasks, negative feedback control was adopted based on the deviation between the actual trajectory and the expected trajectory, and good control performance was achieved through simple parameter debugging. For instance, a robust controller was put forward in[11](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR11 "Luo, X., Mu, D., Wang, Z., Ning, P. & Hua, C. Adaptive full-state constrained tracking control for mobile robotic system with unknown dead-zone input. Neurocomputing 524, 31–42 (2023)."), to compensate for the adverse effects of the unknown dead-zone input, which is ubiquitous in mobile robot motors. However, the studies assumed dead zones and saturation with predetermined parameters, while obtaining the parameter values of dead zones and saturation functions was often a challenging task. Besides, when there were multiple constraints and external uncertainties, these methods did not address the difficulties associated with controller design, which may lead to insufficient control performance. Therefore, many scholars have integrated other algorithms into the traditional controllers[12](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR12 "Khosravi, M. A. & Taghirad, H. D. Robust PID control of fully-constrained cable driven parallel robots. Mechatronics 24, 87–97 (2014)."),[13](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR13 "Macfarlane, S. & Croft, E. A. Jerk-bounded manipulator trajectory planning: design for real-time applications. IEEE Trans. Robot. Automat. 19(1), 42–52 (2003)."),[14](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR14 "Jiang, W., Chen, D., Zheng, Y., Qiu, X. & Wang, C. Active disturbance rejection control based on inertia estimation and variable gain for servomechanism of industrial robot. Meas. Control 55(3–4), 189–197 (2022)."). For instance, in[14](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR14 "Jiang, W., Chen, D., Zheng, Y., Qiu, X. & Wang, C. Active disturbance rejection control based on inertia estimation and variable gain for servomechanism of industrial robot. Meas. Control 55(3–4), 189–197 (2022)."), an adaptive controller was developed on basis of linear extended state observer (LESO) for the industrial robot with time-varying load, uncertainties and disturbance. Besides, to improve the accuracy of robotic motion control, many model-based nonlinear control strategies have emerged[15](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR15 "Jiang, W., Chen, D., Zheng, Y., Qiu, X. & Wang, C. Active disturbance rejection control based on inertia estimation and variable gain for servomechanism of industrial robot. Meas. Control 12(17), 8511 (2022)."),[16](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR16 "Zhang, L., Guo, S. & Xi, F. Performance-based assistance control for robot-mediated upper-limbs rehabilitation. Mechatronics 89, 102919 (2023)."),[17](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR17 "Chen, C. et al. Practical prescribed tracking control of n-DOF robotic manipulator with uncertainties via friction compensation approach. Symmetry. 16(4), 423 (2024)."), such as sliding mode control (SMC)[18](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR18 "Chen, Z. et al. RBFNN-based adaptive sliding mode control design for delayed nonlinear multilateral telerobotic system with cooperative manipulation. IEEE Trans. Ind. Inform. 16(2), 1236–1247 (2020)."), adaptive control[19](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR19 "Ouyang, P. R., Zhang, W. J. & Gupta, M. M. An adaptive switching learning control method for trajectory tracking of robot manipulators. Mechatronics 16, 51–61 (2006)."), neural network control (NN)[20](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR20 "Tokuda, F., Arai, S. & Kosuge, K. Convolutional neural network-based visual servoing for eye-to-hand manipulator. IEEE Access 9, 91820–91835 (2021)."), model predictive control (MPC)[21](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR21 "Ginhoux, R. et al. Active filtering of physiological motion in robotized surgery using predictive control. IEEE Trans. Robot. 21, 67–79 (2005)."), fuzzy control[22](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR22 "Zheng, L., Piao, Y., Ma, Y. & Wang, Y. Development and control of articulated amphibious spherical robot. Microsyst. Technol. 26(5), 1553–1561 (2020)."),[23](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR23 "Liu, X., Zhao, T., Cao, J. & Li, P. Design and prediction of self-organizing interval type-2 fuzzy wavelet neural network. Inf. Sci. 661, 120157 (2024)."), and auto-disturbance rejection control (ADRC)[24](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR24 "Li, H. Active disturbance rejection control for a fluid driven hand rehabilitation device. IEEE/ASME Trans. Mechatron. 26(2), 841–853 (2021)."). For instance, in[16](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR16 "Zhang, L., Guo, S. & Xi, F. Performance-based assistance control for robot-mediated upper-limbs rehabilitation. Mechatronics 89, 102919 (2023)."), a Takagi–Sugeno (T–S) fuzzy model was employed to reconstruct the original nonlinear systems, and a hybrid-driven mechanism was applied for filter design to balance the systems' performance and the limited network resource consumption. In[22](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR22 "Zheng, L., Piao, Y., Ma, Y. & Wang, Y. Development and control of articulated amphibious spherical robot. Microsyst. Technol. 26(5), 1553–1561 (2020)."), a fuzzy controller was designed to solve the control accuracy problem of a spherical robot under a complex underwater environment. In[23](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR23 "Liu, X., Zhao, T., Cao, J. & Li, P. Design and prediction of self-organizing interval type-2 fuzzy wavelet neural network. Inf. Sci. 661, 120157 (2024)."), a self-organizing interval type-2 fuzzy wavelet neural network-based controller was designed for predicting and identifying nonlinear systems to improve the interaction performance. Among these controllers, as an effective robust control method, ADRC can effectively solve the control problems of nonlinear and uncertain systems[25](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR25 "Khaled, T. A., Akhrif, O. & Bonev, I. A. Dynamic path correction of an industrial robot using a distance sensor and an ADRC controller. IEEE/ASME Trans. Mechatron. 26(3), 1646–1656 (2020)."),[26](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR26 "Zheng Q., Gao L., Gao Z. On Stability Analysis of Active Disturbance Rejection Control for Nonlinear Time-Varying Plants with Unknown Dynamics. In Proceedings of the 46th IEEE Conference on Decision and Control, New Orleans, LA, USA, pp. 3501–3506, 2007."),[27](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR27 "Garran, P. T. & Garcia, G. Design of an optimal PID controller for a coupled tanks system employing ADRC. IEEE Latin Am. Trans. 15, 189–196 (2017)."), since it was not only independent with the model, also had strong robustness and adaptability[28](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR28 "Aole, S., Elamvazuthi, I., Waghmar, L., Patre, B. & Meriaudeau, F. Improved active disturbance rejection control for trajectory tracking control of lower limb robotic rehabilitation exoskeleton. Sensors 20(13), 3681 (2020)."). Besides, the internal disturbances, i.e., model, parameter perturbations and unmeasurable external disturbances, was attributed to the total disturbance, which can be observed by an extended state observer in real-time. In this way, the approximate linearization and deterministic linearization of nonlinear uncertain systems was achieved[29](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR29 "Gao Z., Hu S., Jiang, F. A novel motion control design approach based on active disturbance rejection. In Proceedings of the 40th IEEE Conference on Decision and Control, Orlando, FL, USA, 4–7 Dec. 2001; vol. 5, pp. 4877–4882."). Therefore, the ADRC can effectively improve the dynamic tracking control performance and robustness of robots, and was widely used in the field of robotic motion control[30](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR30 "Tian G., Gao Z. Benchmark tests of active disturbance rejection control on an industrial motion control platform. In Proceedings of the 2009 American Control Conference, St. Louis, MO, USA, vol. 10, no. 5552–5557, 2009."),[31](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR31 "Su, Y. X. et al. Disturbance-rejection high-precision motion control of a Stewart platform. IEEE Trans. Control Syst. Technol. 12, 364–374 (2004)."),[32](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR32 "Zhu, E. et al. Airship horizontal trajectory tracking control based on active disturbance rejection control (ADRC). Nonlinear Dynamics. 75, 725–734 (2013)."),[33](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR33 "Cheng, X., Tu, X., Zhou, Y. F. & Zhou, R. G. Active disturbance rejection control of multi-joint industrial robots based on dynamic feedforward. Electronics 8(5), 591 (2019)."). In[34](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR34 "Chen, G., Jiang, Y. & Guo, K. Neural active disturbance rejection adaptive lateral manipulation control method for unmanned driving robot. IEEE Intell. Transp. Syst. Mag. 15(1), 387–399 (2023)."), the unknown uncertainties and external disturbances of robot manipulator was investigated by using a novel disturbance observer, which can actively compensate the total disturbance during the trajectory tracking process.

However, the design of the ADRC is relatively complex, involving a large number of variable parameters, and different combinations of parameter values may cause significant variation of the control performance. Moreover, these parameter values have no rules to follow, which means that there is no complete and reliable reference standard form for parameter selection. In the past, most technical personnel relied on empirical testing for selection[34](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR34 "Chen, G., Jiang, Y. & Guo, K. Neural active disturbance rejection adaptive lateral manipulation control method for unmanned driving robot. IEEE Intell. Transp. Syst. Mag. 15(1), 387–399 (2023)."),[35](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR35 "Yao, S., Gao, G., Gao, Z. & Li, S. Active disturbance rejection synchronization control for parallel electro-coating conveyor. ISA Trans. 101, 327–334 (2020)."),[36](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR36 "Xu, H., Tang, H. & Zhao, P. Automatic disturbance rejection control of 6-DOF hydraulic robot. Modul. Mach. Tool Autom. Process. Technol. 3, 87–90 (2020)."). Besides, when adjusting the three parameters and six directions of the robotics, if there were many input variables, the combination of adjustment possibilities will be more. This work was often not competent for non-professionals[36](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR36 "Xu, H., Tang, H. & Zhao, P. Automatic disturbance rejection control of 6-DOF hydraulic robot. Modul. Mach. Tool Autom. Process. Technol. 3, 87–90 (2020)."). In addition, it was easy to produce small amplitude oscillations near the extreme point. With the research of intelligent algorithms, a series of algorithms such as genetic algorithm (GA), ant colony algorithm (ACA), neural network algorithm (NN), particle swarm optimization algorithm (PSO), etc., have begun to be applied to the parameter design of controllers[37](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR37 "Ganguly, S., Sahoo, N. & Das, D. Multi-objective particle swarm optimization based on fuzzy-Pareto-dominance for possibilistic planning of electrical distribution systems incorporating distributed generation. Fuzzy Sets Syst. 213, 47–73 (2013)."),[38](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR38 "Cai Y., Yang S.X. An improved PSO-based approach with dynamic parameter tuning for cooperative target searching of multi-robots, In Proceedings of World Automation Congress, Waikoloa, HI, USA, 2013, pp. 616–621."),[39](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR39 "Kechagiopoulos, P. N. & Beligiannis, G. N. Solving the Urban Transit Routing Problem using a particle swarm optimization-based algorithm. Appl. Soft Comput. 21, 654–676 (2014)."),[40](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR40 "Gao, B., Shen, W., Dai, Y. & Ye, Y. Parameter tuning of auto disturbance rejection controller based on improved glowworm swarm optimization algorithm. Assembly Autom. 42, 427–444 (2022)."). Gao[40](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR40 "Gao, B., Shen, W., Dai, Y. & Ye, Y. Parameter tuning of auto disturbance rejection controller based on improved glowworm swarm optimization algorithm. Assembly Autom. 42, 427–444 (2022).") proposed a glowworm swarm algorithm-based controller to improve the firefly algorithm using sine and cosine functions to accurately and quickly obtain parameters of the ADRC. Among these algorithms, the PSO is one of the most popular intelligent algorithms due to its simple structure, easy implementation and minor code. This algorithm was proposed by Kennedy and Eberhart in 1995, inspired by bird swarm motion[41](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR41 "Kennedy J., Eberhart R., Particle swarm optimization, In: IEEE International Conference on Neural Networks, Perth, Australia, 1995."), and has been successfully applied to many control fields[42](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR42 "Chao, D. et al. Research on active disturbance rejection control with parameter autotune mechanism for induction motors based on adaptive particle swarm optimization algorithm with dynamic inertia weight. IEEE Trans. Power Electron. 34(3), 2841–2855 (2019)."),[43](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR43 "Liu, M., Lin, R., Yang, M., Nazarova, A. V. & Huo, J. Active disturbance rejection motion control of spherical robot with parameter tuning. Ind. Robot 49(2), 332–343 (2022)."),[44](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR44 "Jain, N. K., Nangia, U. & Jain, J. A review of particle swarm optimization. J. Inst. Eng. Ser. B. 9(4), 407–411 (2018)."),[45](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR45 "Ma, T., Guo, X., Su, G., Deng, H. & Yang, T. Research on synchronous control of active disturbance rejection position of multiple hydraulic cylinders of digging-anchor-support robot. Sensors 23(8), 4092 (2023)."). For instance, in[42](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR42 "Chao, D. et al. Research on active disturbance rejection control with parameter autotune mechanism for induction motors based on adaptive particle swarm optimization algorithm with dynamic inertia weight. IEEE Trans. Power Electron. 34(3), 2841–2855 (2019)."), an ADRC of an induction motor was proposed based on a particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm to realize the precise decoupling of the induction motor and the disturbance compensation. In[43](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR43 "Liu, M., Lin, R., Yang, M., Nazarova, A. V. & Huo, J. Active disturbance rejection motion control of spherical robot with parameter tuning. Ind. Robot 49(2), 332–343 (2022)."), the XK-I spherical robot PSO-ADRC motion controller with parameter tuning function was designed by combining the state equation with PSO algorithm. The PSO algorithm was improved by using the genetic algorithm theory to improve the optimization range and convergence rate[45](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#ref-CR45 "Ma, T., Guo, X., Su, G., Deng, H. & Yang, T. Research on synchronous control of active disturbance rejection position of multiple hydraulic cylinders of digging-anchor-support robot. Sensors 23(8), 4092 (2023)."). However, the population diversity was losing during the optimization process, falling into local extremum, and low accuracy in actual practice, scholars have proposed various improvement measures, focusing on the selection of algorithm parameters, or the improvement of algorithm position and speed updating rules. Overall, the above algorithms have improved the PSO from different perspectives, which improved the optimization performance to some extent. However, the problem caused by premature convergence has not been fundamentally solved.

From the analysis, the robotic manipulator is characterized with complex dynamics, and the dynamic model of the robot manipulator is sensitive to the inaccuracies of the system parameters, which extremely increase the difficulties on control stability. Aiming for this problem, we developed an ADRC-based controller that considers both the model uncertainty and disturbance for robust tracking of robotic manipulator. When designing the controller, three main works are made, the main novelties are the following:

1.   (1)
A key point is that an extended state observer is introduced on basis of the dynamic model, to observe the extend state of model uncertainties and external disturbances.

2.   (2)
In combination with the nonlinear control structure of the ADRC controller, the robust trajectory tracking of robotic manipulators is achieved.

3.   (3)
To optimize the key parameters of the controller, an improved particle swarm optimization algorithm (IPSO) was designed using chaos theory, which improves the tracking accuracy of the proposed NDRC strategy effectively.

Finally, using the studies on a two-joint robotic arm, the performance for several commonly used controllers is detailly compared and analyzed under different types of disturbances, which demonstrates the satisfactory control effect of the proposed strategy during the operation processes.

Problem description
-------------------

A robot can be defined as an electromechanical device that can operate autonomously or semi-autonomously to achieve a specific task. As shown in Fig.[1](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-72156-x#Fig1), manipulators consist of a series of links connected by either rotary or translational joints.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Robust control ensures precision and stability in industrial robots, allowing them to handle uncertainties and disturbances, improving efficiency in manufacturing processes.
In autonomous robotics, robust control techniques enable reliable navigation and task execution in dynamic environments, ensuring safety and performance in applications like self-driving cars.
Robust control advancements enhance robotic capabilities, enabling complex tasks and adaptability, leading to innovations in various sectors, including healthcare, logistics, and service industries.
